title: Madaksira hill fort
tagline: Madaksira hill fort — heritage city of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Madakasira Fort, also known as Simhagiri, is a hill fort located in the Madakasira town of Sri Sathya Sai district, in Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Madakasira Fort, also known as Simhagiri, is a hill fort located in the Madakasira town of Sri Sathya Sai district, in Andhra Pradesh, India. The Government of India has designated it as a Monument of National Importance.

topPlaces.name: Madakasira
topPlaces.description: Madakasira is a town with a population 19,432 in 2001 and also a mandal in Sri Satya Sai district in the state of Andhra Pradesh in India. It is located near the state border with Karnataka.

items.note: Madakasira is a town with a population 19,432 in 2001 and also a mandal in Sri Satya Sai district in the state of Andhra Pradesh in India. It is located near the state border with Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Madaksira hill fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Madaksira hill fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Madaksira hill fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (95 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (112 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Madaksira hill fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Madaksira hill fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Madaksira hill fort?
faq.a: Stays in Madaksira hill fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
